title: 8 Herbal Creams For Over Bleeding During Periods
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a natural way to help with heavy bleeding during periods. Some herbs, like Aloe barbadensis, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce bleeding and soothe cramps. Aloe vera is known for its ability to calm and heal irritated tissues, which can help to slow down excessive bleeding.  Another herb, Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has been used for centuries to ease menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relax the uterus and reduce bleeding.  Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, has been traditionally used to treat heavy bleeding and menstrual cramps. It contains flavonoids that can help to reduce bleeding and ease cramps.  Using herbal creams or teas that contain these herbs can help to reduce heavy bleeding and menstrual cramps. When you experience less bleeding, you'll have more energy and can focus on your daily activities without feeling fatigued. You'll also be able to enjoy your favorite activities without feeling weak or uncomfortable.  These herbal creams or teas can be a natural and gentle way to manage your period symptoms. By using them, you can take control of your body and feel more comfortable during your period.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for over bleeding during periods

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains several bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-erythroside B. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce bleeding and discomfort during periods. Aloin, in particular, has been shown to have a vasoconstrictive effect, which means it can help narrow blood vessels and reduce blood flow. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe-emodin and aloe-erythroside B can also help reduce swelling and pain in the uterus, further reducing bleeding. Additionally, aloe barbadensis creams have antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infection and promote wound healing, making it a useful treatment for over bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Then, add the aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil, and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and set at room temperature. Once set, your aloe barbadensis cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce pain and swelling associated with heavy bleeding during periods. The bioactive constituent, gingerol, also has a vasoconstrictive effect, which helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the uterus. This reduction in blood flow can help to slow down heavy bleeding. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale creams may help to reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, further reducing the risk of heavy bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Add 1/2 cup of grated fresh ginger to the saucepan and heat on low for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil with the ginger mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains flavonoids, naphthodianthrones, and phenolic acids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce bleeding and alleviate menstrual cramps. The flavonoids, specifically rutin and quercetin, have a vasoconstrictive effect, causing blood vessels to constrict and reduce blood flow to the affected area. The naphthodianthrones, including hyperforin and hypericin, have a coagulating effect, which helps to stop bleeding quickly. By reducing blood flow and promoting clotting, Hypericum perforatum creams can help to alleviate over-bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum oil, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and add Hypericum perforatum oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and let it cool and harden completely. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams contains several bioactive constituents, including iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have a regulating effect on hormonal activity, which can help to reduce heavy bleeding during periods. Iridoids, specifically aucubin, are known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease cramps and soothe the uterine lining. Flavonoids, such as vitexin, have a balancing effect on estrogen and progesterone levels, which can help to regulate menstrual flow. By addressing hormonal imbalance and inflammation, Vitex agnus-castus creams may provide relief from excessive bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil together in the double boiler over low heat.
Add 10g of dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of jojoba oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the cooled mixture, stirring well to combine. Pour into a container and let it cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhetic acid, glycyrrhizin, and flavonoids, which help with over bleeding during periods. Glycyrrhetic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease cramps in the uterus, while glycyrrhizin has antispasmodic properties that can calm muscle contractions. Flavonoids, on the other hand, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce bleeding. The antiseptic properties of glycyrrhetic acid can also help to prevent infections that can be a complication of heavy bleeding. By reducing inflammation and promoting muscle relaxation, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate over bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of glycerin, 1 cup of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt 1 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add 1 cup of glycerin and 2 cups of distilled water to the melted beeswax. Stir until well combined.
Add 1 tablespoon of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams contains the bioactive constituents triterpenes, alkaloids, and glycosides. These constituents have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bleeding during periods. The astringent properties of triterpenes and glycosides help to constrict blood vessels and reduce menstrual flow. The anti-inflammatory properties of alkaloids reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding. By reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels, Cimicifuga racemosa creams may help to alleviate symptoms of over bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Cimicifuga racemosa herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of Cimicifuga racemosa extract.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the coconut oil and melted beeswax to a mixing bowl. Stir until combined.
Add the distilled water, vitamin E oil, and Cimicifuga racemosa extract to the mixing bowl. Stir until smooth and well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, the Cimicifuga racemosa cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavoglycosides and bilobalide as its active constituents. These compounds help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, which can lead to excessive bleeding during periods. The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba creams also have a vasoconstrictive effect, which means they can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams contain terpenoids, such as bilobalide, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, reducing bleeding. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the severity of menstrual bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in shea butter and Ginkgo biloba extract until well combined.
Add vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour mixture into a clean container and let it cool and harden.
Once hardened, scoop out a small amount and apply to affected area. You can also store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense creams contains a compound called isoflavones, specifically genistein and formononetin, which are known to have a beneficial effect on menstrual bleeding. These isoflavones have a direct action on the body, decreasing inflammation and relaxing the uterine muscle, which can help to reduce heavy bleeding during periods. Additionally, Trifolium pratense creams contain a flavonoid called kaempferol, which has a stabilizing effect on the blood vessels, reducing their permeability and therefore minimizing excessive bleeding. The estrogen-like properties of the isoflavones in Trifolium pratense creams also help to regulate menstrual cycles, reducing the severity of heavy bleeding. By reducing inflammation and regulating the menstrual cycle, Trifolium pratense creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of over bleeding during periods.
remedies.image_alt: Trifolium pratense herbal creams for over bleeding during periods
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Trifolium pratense flowers, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add the dried Trifolium pratense flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix in the melted beeswax and pour the mixture into small tin containers or a lip balm tube to create the creams.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with heavy periods. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and raspberry leaf, have properties that can help regulate menstrual flow. They may also reduce inflammation and calm the uterus, which can lead to less bleeding. However, the effectiveness can vary from person to person, and it's essential to drink these teas regularly to notice any changes.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help with heavy bleeding during periods, but it's not safe to drink them every day. Some teas, like pennyroyal and blue cohosh, can actually worsen bleeding or cause other problems. It's best to limit your intake to when you really need it, like during a particularly heavy day.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like ginger, peppermint, and chamomile can help ease menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding. Some women notice a difference within a few cycles, while others may take longer. It's common for results to appear after 2-3 months of regular consumption, but this can vary from person to person.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for heavy bleeding during periods is best done in the morning. This helps slow down bleeding throughout the day. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and ginger, are especially good at reducing cramps and heavy flow. Drinking them in the morning can give you a sense of relief and comfort for the rest of the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for heavy periods. Some teas, like ginger and saw palmetto, may slow blood clotting, making it harder for your body to stop bleeding. Others, like red clover and dandelion, may thin your blood, also increasing the risk of excessive bleeding. Be cautious when consuming herbal teas with these medications.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to drink herbal teas for over bleeding during periods while pregnant. Some herbal teas can affect the pregnancy, and it's best to be cautious. There are safer alternatives to help manage bleeding, such as heating pads or comfortable clothes. It's always best to choose what's recommended for pregnant women.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for heavy periods may be tempting while breastfeeding, but be cautious. Some herbs can pass into breast milk, affecting the baby. Peppermint and ginger can be safe in small amounts, but excessive consumption may cause digestive issues in the baby. Always check the tea's ingredients and follow the recommended serving size.
